title: System Integration

content.statement: All systems must work as ONE.
content.question: If you picture your life as one machine, where is it most out of sync?
content.equation: Quality = Systems Working Together ÷ Total Systems
sop: Map every major system across your business AND life (health, work, finances, relationships).
Identify where progress in one system is currently undercutting another (e.g. work hours cutting into recovery).
Rate how many Systems Working Together versus your Total Systems (see calculation, page 4)
Pick the single worst conflict and resolve it, adjust timing, ownership, or priority.
Optimize for flow across the whole machine, not the highest score in any one part.
questions: If you picture your life as one machine, where is it most out of sync?
Which part of your business improves while quietly making another part worse?
What would change if you optimized for the whole system instead of the part you're staring at right now?
content.equation_example: Systems Working Together = 4, Total Systems = 6. Quality = 4 ÷ 6 = 67% out of 100%. Higher means most of your systems reinforce each other. Lower means you're running several systems that quietly undercut one another, even while each looks fine on its own.
content.closing_statement: A system optimized alone can still break the whole.

caption:
Principle Nº 149: System Integration

All systems must work as ONE.

Ask yourself: If you picture your life as one machine, where is it most out of sync?
